memory.summary: The scholarship money would really help, but doing it alone feels heavy compared to just having fun right now. I'm worried about starting the essay later.
I keep thinking about the scholarship essay, but sitting with teammates feels so much lighter right now. The pressure to start writing just makes me want to eat lunch instead.

I keep choosing the safety of this easy laughter over the scary work, convincing myself that pushing for progress is just too risky right now.
I'm trapped in this loop of choosing laughter over work because pushing feels too risky, even though my stress is creeping up.

desires: Keep the laughter going; breaking this easy moment feels too risky and might ruin the vibe with my hallmate.
Not feel like a total failure to myself if I just slide into tomorrow instead of facing the scary application right now.
Avoid any internal friction about procrastinating, so I convince myself that 'working on it later' is actually a smart choice.

goals.description: Stay glued to the voice call with cohort_3; let the scholarship essay sit untouched until I feel less alone and the immediate social pressure has dissipated.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive (0.5) overrides the academic value (0.15). The avoidant trait makes me terrified of the vulnerability required to admit I'm stuck, so escaping into the safety of the friend group is the only logical move to lower my stress. My impulsive nature wants instant relief from the 'pressure' now, not later planning.

goals.description: Dive into that casual game stream right now before I have to face the terrifying blank page again.
goals.rationale: I want this immediately because it offers a dopamine hit without friction, but I'll defer it slightly because if I drop the call too abruptly, it might hurt the vibe with cohort_3. I need to finish this laugh session first, then maybe sneak in some gaming later when the guilt feels manageable.

goals.description: Start the scholarship essay now, even if it's just one sentence, to break the cycle of overthinking and avoid feeling like a failure later.
goals.rationale: My high affiliation drive makes me terrified of disappointing others or looking incompetent (like my hallmate would think). The 'avoidant' trait means I'm procrastinating because asking for help feels too vulnerable. My 'impulsive' nature wants immediate fun relief, but starting this tiny bit gives me the instant dopamine hit of 'doing something' without the long-term commitment that stresses me out.

goals.description: Schedule a quick lunch with cohort_3 immediately after typing the first paragraph to distract myself from the pressure.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top value (0.5 weight). Being alone feels heavy and isolating. However, I'm too stressed to commit to a full hour right now; I'll wait until I've made a small dent in the work so I don't feel guilty about leaving it unfinished.

desires: Not have to explain why I'm missing the deadline to my hallmate if they ask
Feel the immediate relief of distraction from the heavy feeling of doing this alone
Avoid the awkwardness of asking for help since it feels like admitting I can't handle it
